rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular aminooxygenation of unactivated alkenes using tethered carbamates/amides as nitrogen sources and TEMPO as the oxygen trap, giving trans-1,2-aminoalkoxyamines.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: TEMPO is oxidized at the RVC anode to the oxoammonium cation, which oxidizes the carbamate/amide N-H to generate a nitrogen-centered radical (with H2O/base accepting the proton); cathode reduces water/H+ to H2.

key_intermediates.name: TEMPO oxoammonium cation (mediator, generated at anode)
carbon-centered radical after 5-exo-trig cyclization onto tethered alkene

electron_flow: At the RVC anode, TEMPO is oxidized to its oxoammonium salt, which abstracts/oxidizes the carbamate or amide N-H (with base/H2O removing the proton) to give a nitrogen-centered radical; this N-radical undergoes 5-exo-trig cyclization onto the tethered unactivated alkene to give a carbon-centered radical, which is trapped by a second equivalent of TEMPO to afford the trans-aminooxygenation product. The cathode evolves H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Trapped the cyclized carbon radical, delivering the alkoxyamine product as a single trans-diastereomer (e.g. 2a in 70% yield).

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl/allyl radical-clock substrate 9 (cinnamyl-type carbamate)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of 9 gave ring-opened/rearranged TEMPO adduct 10 (52%), consistent with a discrete C-centered radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: preformed TEMPO oxoammonium tetrafluoroborate (11)
probe_reagents.observation: With Cs2CO3 as base and 11 (2 equiv) the aminooxygenation product 2a was obtained in 62% yield without electrolysis, supporting oxoammonium as the active oxidant/mediator; no reaction without base or with Na2CO3.

notes: X-ray structures of 2h and 2q confirm trans-diastereoselectivity of the cyclization. The N-radical generation is proposed to proceed via the TEMPO/oxoammonium redox couple acting as an anodic mediator rather than direct SET oxidation of the carbamate N-H; the stoichiometric experiment with oxoammonium salt 11 supports this mediator pathway. Direct anodic oxidation of the N-H substrate cannot be fully excluded as a minor pathway.
